summary: Russell carries momentum from his commanding Austrian victory into his home race at Silverstone, seeking to capitalize on newfound consistency against a resurgent Hamilton, who has already seized pole for the sprint qualifying session in his Ferrari. The radically altered aerodynamics of this year's cars have transformed Silverstone into an unpredictable circuit, creating opportunities for tactical innovation and potential grid reshuffles throughout the sprint-race weekend format. A strong result for either Mercedes or Ferrari this weekend could prove decisive in narrowing the championship gap, with Hamilton's early sprint advantage setting up a crucial test of Russell's maturity under pressure in front of a home crowd.
